slides.title: PhD CET Mock Exam 9 - Research Methodology
slides.subtitle: 50 Practice MCQs for Doctoral Entrance Examination • Topic 1-5

slides.question: Q1: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Descriptive Research**?
options: It is correctly described as: aims to describe the characteristics of a population or phenomenon being studied without manipulating variables.
It has no relation to: describing 'what is'
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a systematic assessment of the worth or merit of a time-bound project, program, or policy.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a category of research design in which the investigation starts after the fact has occurred without interference from the researcher.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Descriptive Research** is accurately described as: aims to describe the characteristics of a population or phenomenon being studied without manipulating variables.

slides.question: Q2: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Exploratory Research**?
options: It has no relation to: problem definition and feasibility
It is correctly described as: is conducted for a research problem when there are few or no earlier studies to refer to, aiming to clarify the exact nature of the problem.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is an observational research method in which data is gathered for the same subjects repeatedly over a period of time.
It is equivalent in purpose to: focuses on identifying similarities and differences between two or more groups, subjects, or datasets.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Exploratory Research** is accurately described as: is conducted for a research problem when there are few or no earlier studies to refer to, aiming to clarify the exact nature of the problem.

slides.question: Q3: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Historical Research**?
options: It is correctly described as: involves studying and interpreting past events to understand current patterns and anticipate future trends.
It is fundamentally characterized by: involves looking at data from a population at one specific point in time to analyze prevalence or differences.
It is equivalent in purpose to: starts with a general theory or hypothesis and tests it through specific empirical observations.
It has no relation to: past event analysis
slides.explanation: Correct! **Historical Research** is accurately described as: involves studying and interpreting past events to understand current patterns and anticipate future trends.

slides.question: Q4: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Empirical Research**?
options: It has no relation to: empirical observation and evidence
It is correctly described as: relies on direct or indirect observation or experience as a database rather than theory or belief.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is an in-depth, detailed study of an individual, group, or event to explore underlying principles in a real-life context.
It is equivalent in purpose to: starts with specific observations or measurements and moves toward broader generalizations and theories.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Empirical Research** is accurately described as: relies on direct or indirect observation or experience as a database rather than theory or belief.

slides.question: Q5: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Theoretical Research**?
options: It is correctly described as: focuses on the development of new theories or abstract models based on existing literature rather than direct experiments.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a qualitative approach that focuses on describing the lived experiences of individuals regarding a specific concept or phenomenon.
It is fundamentally characterized by: establishes cause-and-effect relationships by manipulating an independent variable and observing its effect on a dependent variable.
It has no relation to: abstract model development
slides.explanation: Correct! **Theoretical Research** is accurately described as: focuses on the development of new theories or abstract models based on existing literature rather than direct experiments.

slides.question: Q6: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Analytical Research**?
options: It is correctly described as: uses facts or information already available and analyzes them to make a critical evaluation of the material.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a systematic qualitative methodology that involves the generation of theory from systematic analysis of data.
It has no relation to: critical factual evaluation
It is fundamentally characterized by: determines the frequency with which something occurs or its association with something else, aiming to find causes of a phenomenon.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Analytical Research** is accurately described as: uses facts or information already available and analyzes them to make a critical evaluation of the material.

slides.question: Q7: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Conceptual Research**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a systematic assessment of the worth or merit of a time-bound project, program, or policy.
It is equivalent in purpose to: involves the immersive study of people and cultures in their natural environment over an extended period.
It has no relation to: abstract concept re-interpretation
It is correctly described as: is related to some abstract idea(s) or theory, generally used by philosophers and thinkers to develop new concepts or to re-interpret existing ones.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Conceptual Research** is accurately described as: is related to some abstract idea(s) or theory, generally used by philosophers and thinkers to develop new concepts or to re-interpret existing ones.

slides.question: Q8: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Ex-Post Facto Research**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: focuses on identifying similarities and differences between two or more groups, subjects, or datasets.
It is correctly described as: is a category of research design in which the investigation starts after the fact has occurred without interference from the researcher.
It has no relation to: after-the-fact investigation
It is equivalent in purpose to: aims to expand the existing body of scientific knowledge without any immediate practical application.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Ex-Post Facto Research** is accurately described as: is a category of research design in which the investigation starts after the fact has occurred without interference from the researcher.

slides.question: Q9: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Longitudinal Study**?
options: It is correctly described as: is an observational research method in which data is gathered for the same subjects repeatedly over a period of time.
It is fundamentally characterized by: starts with a general theory or hypothesis and tests it through specific empirical observations.
It has no relation to: long-term repeated observation
It is equivalent in purpose to: is designed to solve practical, real-world problems of modern society.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Longitudinal Study** is accurately described as: is an observational research method in which data is gathered for the same subjects repeatedly over a period of time.

slides.question: Q10: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Cross-Sectional Study**?
options: It has no relation to: single point-in-time snapshot
It is correctly described as: involves looking at data from a population at one specific point in time to analyze prevalence or differences.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a reflective process of progressive problem-solving led by individuals working with others in teams to address immediate issues.
It is fundamentally characterized by: starts with specific observations or measurements and moves toward broader generalizations and theories.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Cross-Sectional Study** is accurately described as: involves looking at data from a population at one specific point in time to analyze prevalence or differences.

slides.question: Q11: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Type II Error**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a clear, precise statement identifying an area of concern, a condition to be improved, or a difficulty to be eliminated.
It has no relation to: false negative (accepting false H0)
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a research design that shares similarities with traditional experimental design but lacks random assignment to groups.
It is correctly described as: occurs when the null hypothesis is accepted when it is actually false (false negative), denoted by beta.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Type II Error** is accurately described as: occurs when the null hypothesis is accepted when it is actually false (false negative), denoted by beta.

slides.question: Q12: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Independent Variable**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is an experimental procedure in which neither the participants nor the researchers know who is receiving a particular treatment.
It has no relation to: manipulated variable
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a visual or written product that explains, either graphically or in narrative form, the main things to be studied.
It is correctly described as: is the variable that is manipulated or controlled by the researcher to observe its effect.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Independent Variable** is accurately described as: is the variable that is manipulated or controlled by the researcher to observe its effect.

slides.question: Q13: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Dependent Variable**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a small-scale preliminary study conducted to evaluate feasibility, time, cost, and adverse events prior to a full-scale research project.
It has no relation to: measured outcome variable
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the probability that a statistical test will correctly reject a false null hypothesis, equal to 1 minus beta.
It is correctly described as: is the variable being tested and measured, which changes in response to the independent variable.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Dependent Variable** is accurately described as: is the variable being tested and measured, which changes in response to the independent variable.

slides.question: Q14: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Extraneous Variable**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: specifies the expected direction of the relationship or difference between variables (e.g., group A will score higher than B).
It is correctly described as: is any variable other than the independent variable that could influence the results of the study.
It has no relation to: uncontrolled confounding variable
It is fundamentally characterized by: is selected when the primary objective is to gain insights and familiarity with a problem for more precise investigation later.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Extraneous Variable** is accurately described as: is any variable other than the independent variable that could influence the results of the study.

slides.question: Q15: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Confounding Variable**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is chosen to provide an accurate portrayal of characteristics of a particular individual, situation, or group.
It is correctly described as: is an extraneous variable that correlates with both the independent and dependent variables, masking the true relationship.
It has no relation to: relationship-masking variable
It is equivalent in purpose to: predicts that there is a relationship or difference, but does not specify which group will be higher or lower.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Confounding Variable** is accurately described as: is an extraneous variable that correlates with both the independent and dependent variables, masking the true relationship.

slides.question: Q16: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Control Variable**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is the group in an experiment that does not receive the treatment or manipulation, serving as a baseline for comparison.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the extent to which a study establishes a trustworthy cause-and-effect relationship, free from confounding variables.
It is correctly described as: is a variable that is kept constant or controlled throughout the experiment to prevent it from influencing the outcome.
It has no relation to: constant baseline variable
slides.explanation: Correct! **Control Variable** is accurately described as: is a variable that is kept constant or controlled throughout the experiment to prevent it from influencing the outcome.

slides.question: Q17: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Literature Review**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a research design that shares similarities with traditional experimental design but lacks random assignment to groups.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the extent to which the results of a study can be generalized to other situations, people, and times.
It has no relation to: critical gap analysis of past works
It is correctly described as: is a comprehensive summary of previous research on a specific topic, identifying gaps in current knowledge.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Literature Review** is accurately described as: is a comprehensive summary of previous research on a specific topic, identifying gaps in current knowledge.

slides.question: Q18: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Research Problem**?
options: It has no relation to: clear problem identification
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the conceptual blueprint or framework within which research is conducted.
It is correctly described as: is a clear, precise statement identifying an area of concern, a condition to be improved, or a difficulty to be eliminated.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is an experimental procedure in which neither the participants nor the researchers know who is receiving a particular treatment.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Research Problem** is accurately described as: is a clear, precise statement identifying an area of concern, a condition to be improved, or a difficulty to be eliminated.

slides.question: Q19: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Conceptual Framework**?
options: It is correctly described as: is a visual or written product that explains, either graphically or in narrative form, the main things to be studied.
It has no relation to: conceptual schema explanation
It is fundamentally characterized by: is the probability that a statistical test will correctly reject a false null hypothesis, equal to 1 minus beta.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a tentative, testable statement about the relationship between two or more variables.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Conceptual Framework** is accurately described as: is a visual or written product that explains, either graphically or in narrative form, the main things to be studied.

slides.question: Q20: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Pilot Study**?
options: It has no relation to: feasibility and scale trial
It is equivalent in purpose to: asserts that there is no significant difference or relationship between the variables being tested.
It is fundamentally characterized by: specifies the expected direction of the relationship or difference between variables (e.g., group A will score higher than B).
It is correctly described as: is a small-scale preliminary study conducted to evaluate feasibility, time, cost, and adverse events prior to a full-scale research project.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Pilot Study** is accurately described as: is a small-scale preliminary study conducted to evaluate feasibility, time, cost, and adverse events prior to a full-scale research project.

slides.question: Q21: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Multi-Stage Sampling**?
options: It has no relation to: complex multi-stage unit selection
It is correctly described as: involves selecting samples in stages using smaller and smaller sampling units at each stage.
It is fundamentally characterized by: occurs when some members of a population are systematically more likely to be selected in a sample than others.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a scaling method that measures the psychological meaning of concepts using bipolar adjectives (e.g., Good-Bad).
slides.explanation: Correct! **Multi-Stage Sampling** is accurately described as: involves selecting samples in stages using smaller and smaller sampling units at each stage.

slides.question: Q22: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Non-Probability Sampling**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is data collected firsthand by the researcher specifically for the current research project.
It has no relation to: convenience and subjectivity selection
It is correctly described as: does not use random selection, meaning some members of the population have zero chance of selection.
It is equivalent in purpose to: involves gathering primary data by systematically watching and recording behavioral patterns of people, objects, or occurrences.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Non-Probability Sampling** is accurately described as: does not use random selection, meaning some members of the population have zero chance of selection.

slides.question: Q23: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Convenience Sampling**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is a qualitative technique where a small group of people (usually 6-10) discuss a topic guided by a moderator.
It has no relation to: accessibility-based collection
It is fundamentally characterized by: is pre-existing data collected by others for a different purpose, such as census data or published articles.
It is correctly described as: selects samples that are easiest to access or recruit, resulting in high speed but potential bias.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Convenience Sampling** is accurately described as: selects samples that are easiest to access or recruit, resulting in high speed but potential bias.

slides.question: Q24: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Quota Sampling**?
options: It is correctly described as: selects a specified number of elements from defined subgroups, but using non-random selection methods.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a research tool used to determine the presence of certain words, themes, or concepts within qualitative data.
It has no relation to: non-random strata filling
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a structured research instrument consisting of a series of questions for gathering information from respondents.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Quota Sampling** is accurately described as: selects a specified number of elements from defined subgroups, but using non-random selection methods.

slides.question: Q25: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Purposive Sampling**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is the use of multiple data sources or collection methods to study a single phenomenon to increase validity.
It has no relation to: judgmental target selection
It is correctly described as: relies on the researcher's judgment to select elements that are most representative or informative for the study.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is an interview method where all questions are pre-determined and asked in exactly the same order for all respondents.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Purposive Sampling** is accurately described as: relies on the researcher's judgment to select elements that are most representative or informative for the study.

slides.question: Q26: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Snowball Sampling**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is the percentage of people who completed the research instrument out of the total sample selected.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is an interval scale used to measure attitudes by asking respondents to specify their level of agreement on a symmetric agree-disagree scale.
It has no relation to: referral-based chain network
It is correctly described as: relies on existing research participants to recruit future participants from among their acquaintances.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Snowball Sampling** is accurately described as: relies on existing research participants to recruit future participants from among their acquaintances.

slides.question: Q27: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Sampling Frame Error**?
options: It is correctly described as: occurs when the list from which the sample is drawn does not accurately represent the target population.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a scaling method that measures the psychological meaning of concepts using bipolar adjectives (e.g., Good-Bad).
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the trial administration of a questionnaire to a small group to detect and fix ambiguities before main collection.
It has no relation to: frame-population mismatch error
slides.explanation: Correct! **Sampling Frame Error** is accurately described as: occurs when the list from which the sample is drawn does not accurately represent the target population.

slides.question: Q28: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Sampling Bias**?
options: It is correctly described as: occurs when some members of a population are systematically more likely to be selected in a sample than others.
It has no relation to: systematic selection skew
It is fundamentally characterized by: involves gathering primary data by systematically watching and recording behavioral patterns of people, objects, or occurrences.
It is equivalent in purpose to: gives every element in the population an equal, non-zero chance of being selected.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Sampling Bias** is accurately described as: occurs when some members of a population are systematically more likely to be selected in a sample than others.

slides.question: Q29: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Primary Data**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a qualitative technique where a small group of people (usually 6-10) discuss a topic guided by a moderator.
It is correctly described as: is data collected firsthand by the researcher specifically for the current research project.
It has no relation to: original direct collection
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a probability sampling method where every member of the population has an equal chance of selection.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Primary Data** is accurately described as: is data collected firsthand by the researcher specifically for the current research project.

slides.question: Q30: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Secondary Data**?
options: It has no relation to: previously collected material
It is correctly described as: is pre-existing data collected by others for a different purpose, such as census data or published articles.
It is equivalent in purpose to: divides the population into homogeneous subgroups (strata) and then samples randomly from each stratum.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a research tool used to determine the presence of certain words, themes, or concepts within qualitative data.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Secondary Data** is accurately described as: is pre-existing data collected by others for a different purpose, such as census data or published articles.

slides.question: Q31: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Pearson Correlation**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a parametric test used to compare the means of exactly two groups, either independent or paired.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a symmetric, bell-shaped probability distribution where the mean, median, and mode are all equal.
It has no relation to: linear correlation index
It is correctly described as: measures the strength and direction of a linear relationship between two continuous variables.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Pearson Correlation** is accurately described as: measures the strength and direction of a linear relationship between two continuous variables.

slides.question: Q32: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Spearman Rank Correlation**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is a distribution with a tail extending toward the right side (higher values), where Mean > Median > Mode.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a statistical test used to compare means when the sample size is large (n > 30) and population variance is known.
It has no relation to: monotonic rank relationship score
It is correctly described as: is a non-parametric measure of rank correlation, assessing how well a relationship can be described using a monotonic function.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Spearman Rank Correlation** is accurately described as: is a non-parametric measure of rank correlation, assessing how well a relationship can be described using a monotonic function.

slides.question: Q33: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Regression Coefficient**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is a distribution with a tail extending toward the left side (lower values), where Mean < Median < Mode.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is the probability of rejecting the null hypothesis when it is true, commonly set at 0.05.
It has no relation to: slope relationship prediction
It is correctly described as: represents the rate of change in the dependent variable for every unit change in the independent variable.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Regression Coefficient** is accurately described as: represents the rate of change in the dependent variable for every unit change in the independent variable.

slides.question: Q34: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Parametric Test**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: measures the tail-heaviness or peakedness of a probability distribution relative to a normal distribution.
It has no relation to: distribution-assumed statistical testing
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a range of values derived from sample statistics that is likely to contain the true population parameter.
It is correctly described as: makes strict assumptions about the parameters of the population distribution (e.g., normality).
slides.explanation: Correct! **Parametric Test** is accurately described as: makes strict assumptions about the parameters of the population distribution (e.g., normality).

slides.question: Q35: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Non-Parametric Test**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is a non-parametric alternative to the independent t-test used to compare two independent groups.
It is fundamentally characterized by: indicates the number of values in the final calculation of a statistic that are free to vary.
It has no relation to: distribution-free statistical testing
It is correctly described as: does not make assumptions about the underlying population distribution, suitable for ordinal or skewed data.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Non-Parametric Test** is accurately described as: does not make assumptions about the underlying population distribution, suitable for ordinal or skewed data.

slides.question: Q36: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Chi-Square Test**?
options: It has no relation to: categorical association verification
It is fundamentally characterized by: measures the standard deviation of the sample mean's estimate of the population mean.
It is correctly described as: is a non-parametric test used to determine if there is a significant association between two categorical variables.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a non-parametric alternative to the paired t-test used to compare two related samples.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Chi-Square Test** is accurately described as: is a non-parametric test used to determine if there is a significant association between two categorical variables.

slides.question: Q37: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **ANOVA (Analysis of Variance)**?
options: It has no relation to: comparing three or more group means
It is correctly described as: is a parametric test used to compare the means of three or more independent groups.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a symmetric, bell-shaped probability distribution where the mean, median, and mode are all equal.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a non-parametric alternative to one-way ANOVA used to compare three or more independent groups.
slides.explanation: Correct! **ANOVA (Analysis of Variance)** is accurately described as: is a parametric test used to compare the means of three or more independent groups.

slides.question: Q38: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Student's t-Test**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a distribution with a tail extending toward the right side (higher values), where Mean > Median > Mode.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the mathematical average of a dataset, calculated by summing all values and dividing by the count.
It has no relation to: comparing two group means
It is correctly described as: is a parametric test used to compare the means of exactly two groups, either independent or paired.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Student's t-Test** is accurately described as: is a parametric test used to compare the means of exactly two groups, either independent or paired.

slides.question: Q39: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Z-Test**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a distribution with a tail extending toward the left side (lower values), where Mean < Median < Mode.
It is correctly described as: is a statistical test used to compare means when the sample size is large (n > 30) and population variance is known.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the middle value of an ordered dataset, dividing it into two equal halves, unaffected by outliers.
It has no relation to: large-sample mean comparison
slides.explanation: Correct! **Z-Test** is accurately described as: is a statistical test used to compare means when the sample size is large (n > 30) and population variance is known.

slides.question: Q40: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Level of Significance**?
options: It has no relation to: alpha threshold for hypothesis rejection
It is correctly described as: is the probability of rejecting the null hypothesis when it is true, commonly set at 0.05.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the most frequently occurring value in a dataset.
It is fundamentally characterized by: measures the tail-heaviness or peakedness of a probability distribution relative to a normal distribution.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Level of Significance** is accurately described as: is the probability of rejecting the null hypothesis when it is true, commonly set at 0.05.

slides.question: Q41: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Intellectual Property Rights (IPR)**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is a specialist work of writing on a single scholarly subject or aspect of a subject, usually by a single author.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is the evaluation of creative work or performance by other people in the same field in order to maintain quality.
It is correctly described as: are legal rights governing the protection of creations of the mind, including patents, copyrights, and trademarks.
It has no relation to: legal protection of mental creations
slides.explanation: Correct! **Intellectual Property Rights (IPR)** is accurately described as: are legal rights governing the protection of creations of the mind, including patents, copyrights, and trademarks.

slides.question: Q42: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Patent**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is the process of indexing publications by their citations to track scientific discourse and calculate metrics.
It has no relation to: monopoly right for technical invention
It is fundamentally characterized by: refers to online, free-of-charge access to peer-reviewed scholarly research, removing price and permission barriers.
It is correctly described as: is an exclusive right granted for an invention, providing the owner with the right to exclude others from making or selling it for a limited period.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Patent** is accurately described as: is an exclusive right granted for an invention, providing the owner with the right to exclude others from making or selling it for a limited period.

slides.question: Q43: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Copyright**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is a list of all sources consulted during the research process, regardless of whether they were directly cited in the text.
It has no relation to: protection of expression of ideas
It is correctly described as: is a legal right that grants the creator of an original work exclusive rights for its use and distribution.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the unethical practice of inventing data or results and recording or reporting them as true research outcomes.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Copyright** is accurately described as: is a legal right that grants the creator of an original work exclusive rights for its use and distribution.

slides.question: Q44: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Impact Factor**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: is the unethical practice of manipulating research materials, equipment, or processes, or changing or omitting data.
It is correctly described as: is a measure of the frequency with which the average article in a journal has been cited in a particular year.
It has no relation to: journal citation frequency index
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a self-contained, brief summary of a research paper, thesis, or review, outlining goals, methods, and main findings.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Impact Factor** is accurately described as: is a measure of the frequency with which the average article in a journal has been cited in a particular year.

slides.question: Q45: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **H-Index**?
options: It is equivalent in purpose to: occurs when an investigator's private interests or relationships compromise the professional judgment of research.
It has no relation to: citation and publication volume balance metric
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a note placed at the bottom of a page of a document that provides additional context or citations for text on that page.
It is correctly described as: is an author-level metric that measures both the productivity and citation impact of the publications of a scientist.
slides.explanation: Correct! **H-Index** is accurately described as: is an author-level metric that measures both the productivity and citation impact of the publications of a scientist.

slides.question: Q46: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Institutional Review Board (IRB)**?
options: It has no relation to: human subject safety oversight board
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the portion of a research paper dedicated to thanking individuals, funders, or institutions who aided the study.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a structured document outlining what the researcher plans to study, why it is important, and how they will execute it.
It is correctly described as: is a committee formally designated to approve, monitor, and review biomedical and behavioral research involving humans.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Institutional Review Board (IRB)** is accurately described as: is a committee formally designated to approve, monitor, and review biomedical and behavioral research involving humans.

slides.question: Q47: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Informed Consent**?
options: It has no relation to: voluntary participant agreement process
It is correctly described as: is the process of providing research participants with clear info about the study to help them voluntarily agree to participate.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is a specialist work of writing on a single scholarly subject or aspect of a subject, usually by a single author.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the standardized sequence of chapters (Introduction, Literature, Methods, Results, Discussion) for reporting research.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Informed Consent** is accurately described as: is the process of providing research participants with clear info about the study to help them voluntarily agree to participate.

slides.question: Q48: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Peer Review**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is the process of indexing publications by their citations to track scientific discourse and calculate metrics.
It is equivalent in purpose to: is the practice of taking someone else's work or ideas and passing them off as one's own without proper citation.
It has no relation to: expert quality check before publication
It is correctly described as: is the evaluation of creative work or performance by other people in the same field in order to maintain quality.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Peer Review** is accurately described as: is the evaluation of creative work or performance by other people in the same field in order to maintain quality.

slides.question: Q49: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Open Access**?
options: It is fundamentally characterized by: is the unethical practice of inventing data or results and recording or reporting them as true research outcomes.
It has no relation to: barrier-free research accessibility
It is equivalent in purpose to: occurs when an author reuses portions of their own previously published work without proper attribution.
It is correctly described as: refers to online, free-of-charge access to peer-reviewed scholarly research, removing price and permission barriers.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Open Access** is accurately described as: refers to online, free-of-charge access to peer-reviewed scholarly research, removing price and permission barriers.

slides.question: Q50: Which of the following statements is **TRUE** regarding **Bibliography**?
options: It is correctly described as: is a list of all sources consulted during the research process, regardless of whether they were directly cited in the text.
It is fundamentally characterized by: is the unethical practice of manipulating research materials, equipment, or processes, or changing or omitting data.
It has no relation to: consulted sources comprehensive list
It is equivalent in purpose to: is a citation and formatting style established by the American Psychological Association, commonly used in social sciences.
slides.explanation: Correct! **Bibliography** is accurately described as: is a list of all sources consulted during the research process, regardless of whether they were directly cited in the text.
